LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT CALEB YI, DBA PINIX REALTY'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND IS CONTINUED TO JULY 7, 2017 IN ORDER TO ALLOW THE DEFENDANT AN OPPORTUNITY TO FILE A NEW DECLARATION WITH THE PROPOSED ANSWER ATTACHED BY JULY 6, 2017. MR. YI TO SERVE OPPOSING COUNSEL BY WAY OF E-MAIL AND TO SEND A COURTESY COPY TO THE COURT BY SENDING IT TO THE CONTEST E-MAIL ADDRESS OF THE COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: CAROL KAREN, CSR #8189

MINI MINUTES FOR JUL-07-2017 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT CALEB YI, DBA PINIX REALTY'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT AND DEFAULT JUDGMENT IS GRANTED. MR. YI HAS SHOWN SUFFICIENT EVIDENCE FOR EXCUSABLE MISTAKE AND NEGLECT PER CCP 473(B). (SHAMBLIN V. BRATTAN (1988) 44 CAL. 3D 474, 478 (BECAUSE OF CALIFORNIA'S STRONG POLICY OF DECIDING CASES ON THEIR MERITS, A PARTY WHO TIMELY MOVES TO SET ASIDE A DEFAULT NEED ONLY SHOW "VERY SLIGHT EVIDENCE" TO OBTAIN THE RELIEF SOUGHT)). MR. YI HAS ALSO REMEDIED HIS INITIAL FAILURE TO RILE A PROPOSED ANSWER. MR. YI MUST FILE HIS ANSWER NO LATER THAN JULY 17, 2017. PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER THAT IS VERBATIM WITH THE COURT'S RULING AND IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, COURT REPORTER: CAROL KAREN, CSR	711, CKAREN@SFTC.ORG =(302/HK)

REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, PLAINTIFF FOGHORN REALTY INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT AGAINST DEFENDANT CALEB YI DBA PINIX REALTY IS CONTINUED TO MARCH 1, 2018 ON COURT'S OWN MOTION. JUDGE: ANGELA BRADSTREET; CLERK: R.WONG; NOT REPORTED. (501)
ORDER GRANTING PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT AGINST DEFENDANT CALEB DBA PINIX REALTY (ENDORSED COPY MAILED)
MINI MINUTES FOR MAR-01-2018 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 501

REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, ORDER AND JUDGEMENT IS SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. THE COURT ADOPTS THE TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: PLAINTIFF FOGHORN REALTY INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT AGAINST DEFENDANT CALEB YI DBA PINIX REALTY IS GRANTED. MOVING PARTY SHIFTED ITS BURDEN AND OPPOSING PARTY FAILED TO CREATE A TRIABLE ISSUE OF MATERIAL FACT WITH COMPETENT ADMISSIBLE EVIDENCE. FACT 16, WHILE DISPUTED AND SUPPORTED BY EVIDENCE, IS NOT A MATERIAL FACT AS TO THE CAUSE OF ACTION FOR BREACH OF CONTRACT. IT IS CITED IN SUPPORT OF ARGUMENT REGARDING LACHES, WHICH IS NOT A PROPER DEFENSE IN THIS ACTION FOR MONEY DAMAGES AND NO EVIDENCE OF PREJUDICE PROVIDED. 9:55 AM 2ND CALLING OF THE CASE. JUDGE: ANNE-CHRISTINE MASSULLO; CLERK: R.WONG; NOT REPORTED. (501)

THE COURT ORDERED THE FOLLOWING JUDGMENT ENTERED: IT IS ADJUDGED THAT PLAINTIFF FOGHORN REALTY INC. RECOVER FROM DEFENDANT CALEB YI DBA PINIX REALTY $30,000.00 PRINCIPAL, $6,990.00 INTEREST, TOTAL JUDGMENT $36,990.00, SEE SCANNED DOC

Off Calendar Mar-08-2018 Vacated

Plaintiff Foghorn Realty Inc.'S Motion For Summary Judgment Against Defendant Caleb Yi Dba Pinix Realty.
Off Calendar Mar-01-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
(415-421-0100) Steven Williams for Plaintiff/ opposing party .
No other appearances.

Order and Judgement is signed in open court. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows: Plaintiff Foghorn Realty Inc.'s Motion For Summary Judgment Against Defendant Caleb Yi Dba Pinix Realty is GRANTED. Moving party shifted its burden and opposing party failed to create a triable issue of material fact with competent admissible evidence. Fact 16, while disputed and supported by evidence, is not a material fact as to the cause of action for breach of contract. It is cited in support of argument regarding laches, which is not a proper defense in this action for money damages and no evidence of prejudice provided. 9:55 AM 2nd calling of the case. Judge: Anne-Christine Massullo; Clerk: R.Wong; Not Reported. (501)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Against Defendant Caleb Yi Dba Pinix Realty
Off Calendar Feb-09-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances.

PLAINTIFF FOGHORN REALTY INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Against Defendant Caleb Yi Dba Pinix Realty is TRANSFERRED to department 501 to be heard on the same date at the same time. That department handles all cases related to the acquisition of real property per SF Local Rule 8.10A1.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer; Clerk: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: not reported (L&M302)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Aug-23-2017 Set For Trial On Mar-12-18 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Defendant Caleb Yi, Dba Pinix Realty'S Motion To Set Aside Default/Default Judgment/Leave To Defend
Off Calendar Jul-07-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling with amendment as follows:

Defendant Caleb Yi, Dba Pinix Realty'S Motion To Set Aside Default and Default Judgment is
GRANTED. Mr. Yi has shown sufficient evidence for excusable mistake and neglect per CCP 473(b). (Shamblin v. Brattan (1988) 44 Cal. 3d 474, 478 (Because of California's strong policy of deciding cases on their merits, a party who timely moves to set aside a default need only show "very slight evidence" to obtain the relief sought)). Mr. Yi has also remedied his initial failure to rile a proposed answer. Mr. Yi must file his answer no later than July 17, 2017. Prevailing party to submit a proposed order that is verbatim with the Court's ruling and in compliance with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR 711, ckaren@sftc.org =(302/HK)

MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND

MINI MINUTES:

CALEB YI, TEL. NO. 1-510-504-2506, DEFENDANT APPEARING IN PRO PER.

RULING - DEFENDANT CALEB YI, DBA PINIX REALTY'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND IS CONTINUED TO JULY 7, 2017 IN ORDER TO ALLOW THE DEFENDANT AN OPPORTUNITY TO FILE A NEW DECLARATION WITH THE PROPOSED ANSWER ATTACHED BY JULY 6, 2017. MR. YI TO SERVE OPPOSING COUNSEL BY WAY OF E-MAIL AND TO SEND A COURTESY COPY TO THE COURT BY SENDING IT TO THE CONTEST E-MAIL ADDRESS OF THE COURT. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR #8189

MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar May-19-2017 Vacated

Notice Of Hearing Re Prove Up Default Judgment
Off Calendar May-11-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly as scheduled this day for Default Prove-Up Hearing.
Dept. 514, Judge: Stephen M. Murphy, Clerk: J. Bertelsen, Reporter: Mitch Lyon, CSR #6840

Steven C. Williams, Esq., appearing for Plaintiff, Foghorn Realty Inc.
No appearance by defaulted defendant Caleb Yi d.b.a. Pinix Realty.
Default of Defendant has been duly entered.
Witness Priscilla Stoyanof sworn and examined on behalf of Plaintiff.
After Evidence and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of Plaintiff and against Defaulted Defendant as follows:

Damages: $30,000.00
Pre-Judgment Interest: $4,710.06
Attorney's Fees: $0
Costs: $570.00
Total Judgment in the amount of $35,280.06
Judgment signed in open court.

Notice Of Hearing Re Prove Up Default Judgment
Off Calendar Apr-18-2017 Continued To May-11-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 514

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Dec-02-2016 Set For Trial On Jun-26-17 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-13-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances.

The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANT CHICAGO TITLE COMPANY'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT, as to the second cause of action for breach of fiduciary duty in the complaint, is SUSTAINED with twenty days leave to amend. Plaintiff Foghorn Realty, Inc. does not allege that Chicago Title agreed to be bound by or carry out the escrow instructions in the purchase agreement. Absent such an allegation, Chicago Title has no duty of any kind to adhere to those escrow instructions. If Foghorn Realty can do so in good faith, it is granted leave to amend to make such an allegation. Written order is signed and filed in open court.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Reporter: Not Reported. (D302)
